title: pavetta travancorica
intro: Pavetta travancorica, also known as the Travancore Pavetta or the Indian Pink, is a flowering plant species native to the Western Ghats region of India. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and flowers, which are used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders. The health benefits of Pavetta travancorica include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and protect the body from oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as decoctions and infusions, which are often consumed as a tea or applied topically to the affected area.
